prompt: A standing statue of Senju Kannon Bodhisattva reminiscent of ancient Japanese Buddhist sculpture. It features a serene and majestic atmosphere similar to the masterpieces housed in the Tokyo National Museum. A full-body frontal view with a slender and elegant physique, a soft and compassionate expression, half-closed eyes, and a gentle smile. It includes an exquisite crown, flowing celestial robes, and delicate jewelry. A large number of arms are arranged in an orderly radial pattern behind the back to strongly emphasize the symbolism of the Thousand-Armed Kannon. The hands may be based on 42 arms in accordance with actual sculptural traditions. It stands on a lotus pedestal with a halo behind. The material is primarily wood carving, depicting historical textures such as lacquer, peeling gold leaf, weathered colors from aging, traces of original color, cracks, wear, and a sense of temple heritage. The overall design evokes Japanese Buddhist sculptures of the Heian to Kamakura periods, characterized by dignity, nobility, and religious solemnity. Like a professional photograph of a museum collection, the background is dark and subdued, highlighting the three-dimensionality and texture of the statue. Museum-grade, cultural asset-grade, ultra-high definition.
